reasoning: The Nigerian Tax Reform Bills, which include the exemption of real estate transactions from VAT, have significant momentum as of May 5, 2025. The bills were passed by the House of Representatives on March 13, 2025, after addressing controversial provisions such as the VAT derivation formula (capping it at 30% to appease Northern states). The Senate, having already passed the bills for a second reading in late 2024 and completed public hearings in early 2025, is scheduled to resume on May 6, 2025, with these reforms as a top priority. Taiwo Oyedele, the reform committee chairman, has repeatedly stated a target implementation date of July 1, 2025, and continued to promote the real estate VAT exemption in late April 2025. While legislative delays are common and the original Q1 enactment target was missed, the current alignment of the executive and both legislative chambers, combined with the clear target date, makes enactment before July 1, 2025, likely.
